reasoning_model_1: The model completely failed to follow the provided script. The user requested: "Wuwu... the painting I worked hard on all afternoon was torn to shreds by the puppy; I'm so sad. All my hard work was in vain; I don't want to paint anymore." The model generated a completely different, paraphrased text: "Oh no, the painting I spent all afternoon on torn to shreds by the puppy? I'm crying, I really am..." (00:00-00:15). Due to this hallucination and significant deviation from the core content, the audio is scored 0 based on the content accuracy criterion.
reasoning_model_2: The model followed the script accurately, with one specific interpretation: it converted the onomatopoeia "Wuwu..." into the spoken phrase "Boo hoo" (00:00-00:02). While the prompt requested "Non-Linguistic Vocalizations" (crying sounds), the model synthesized the linguistic representation of crying ("Boo hoo") rather than generating a natural sobbing sound effect. The remaining text (00:02-00:17) was read accurately with a distinct sad and complaining tone. Although the expression of crying was stylized and cartoonish rather than realistic, the model adhered to the content and attempted the emotional tone requested.
model_comparison: Model 1 (T1) receives a score of 0 because it completely hallucinated the text, failing the fundamental requirement of content accuracy. It did not read the requested sentence. Model 2 (T2) adhered to the text significantly better. Regarding the 'Non-Linguistic Vocalizations' feature, T2 interpreted the text 'Wuwu' as the spoken words 'Boo hoo'. While this is a stylized/linguistic approach rather than a realistic non-linguistic sound generation (actual sobbing), it is a valid attempt to convey the meaning of the script's onomatopoeia within a sad prosody. T2 is the clear winner due to T1's critical content failure.
